concerns.text: Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) is violated. The plan states: 'The AMS-02 dataset has no verified URL; thus, the plan will reference the dataset by name only, and the implementation will attempt retrieval... as a fallback'. The Constitution mandates: 'Every external citation... MUST be verified... before contributing review points' and 'A reviewer's score MUST be set to 0.0 if the reviewed artifact has any citation in unreachable or mismatch status'. The plan explicitly accepts an unverified source as a fallback, which contradicts the non-negotiable gate in Principle II.

Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) is violated regarding NOAA/SWPC data. The plan states: 'The AMS-02 dataset has no verified URL... The implementation will attempt retrieval from the official NOAA/SWPC repository'. Similar to AMS-02, the plan accepts a fallback to an unverified source for the solar activity index, violating the requirement that all external citations must be verified against the '# Verified datasets' block before proceeding.

Constitution Principle I (SSoT) check is incomplete. The plan lists 'I. Reproducibility' but does not explicitly map how the 'Single Source of Truth' principle (Principle IV in Constitution) is enforced in the plan steps (e.g., specific mention of hash tracing for figures/statistics in the plan steps, not just the summary). While implied, the plan should explicitly state the mechanism for SSoT compliance in the implementation steps to satisfy the 'Constitution-Check coverage' requirement.
